metrics.name: Governance integrity & liveness
metrics.desc:
Price: $108.77
Max supply: 16M AAVE
Total supply: 16M AAVE
Circulating supply: ~15,182,427 AAVE
Delegated voting power: Full supply (~16M AAVE)
Proposal threshold: 80,000 votes (Level 1) and 2,000,000 votes (Level 2)
Quorum: 320,000 votes (Level 1) and 1,040,000 votes (Level 2)
Top 5 delegate concentration: ~38.5%
Top 10 delegate concentration: Unknown
Quorum hit rate over last 20 proposals: 18
Vote duration: 3 days (Level 1) and 10 days (Level 2)
Proposal delay: 1 day before voting starts

metrics.name: Governance integrity & liveness
metrics.desc:
Governance is clearly live: 18/20 recent proposals hit quorum.
Thresholds and quorum are meaningful in absolute terms, especially for Level 2 actions.
A 1-day proposal delay and multi-day voting windows give some reaction time.
Main weakness is concentration: top 5 delegates control ~38.5%, which is material.
Top 10 concentration is unknown, so decentralization cannot be scored as best-in-class.

metrics.name: Control-plane integrity
metrics.desc:
Number of privileged roles: At least 8 named roles in ACLManager alone: DEFAULT_ADMIN, POOL_ADMIN, RISK_ADMIN, BRIDGE_ROLE, EMERGENCY_ADMIN, ASSET_LISTING_ADMIN, FLASH_BORROWER, plus one additional role ID shown by DeFiScan; there are also further privileged roles in treasury and related governance contracts.
Separation of duties: Yes. Aave Governance separates Level 1 vs Level 2 permissions, uses distinct executors, and docs describe a modular governance architecture across core, voting, and execution networks.
Pausable: Yes. EMERGENCY_ADMIN / POOL_ADMIN can pause reserves or the full pool and disable liquidation grace periods.
Upgradeable: Yes. DeFiScan explicitly flags an upgradeable pool contract and notes governance-controlled upgrades, including governance contract upgrades protected by executor delays.
Timelock delay: 1 day for Level 1 permissions; 7 days for Level 2 permissions.
Timelock bypass allowed: Yes, for emergency actions. Critical permissions are controlled by governance and an Emergency Admin multisig; emergency roles can pause without going through normal proposal execution flow.

metrics.name: Control-plane integrity
metrics.desc:
There is solid structure: separation of duties, dual executors, and timelocks.
7-day delay for higher-sensitivity actions is a strong safeguard.
But the control plane is broad and complex, with many privileged roles.
The system is upgradeable, which increases dependence on governance/security processes.
Emergency roles can bypass normal governance flow for some actions, which weakens integrity.

metrics.name: Treasury custody integrity
metrics.desc:
Value under management: USD 129M
Governed by: Aave DAO governance, with treasury funds held in TreasuryCollector; FUNDS_ADMIN can approve, transfer, create streams, cancel streams, and withdraw from streams.
Timelock delay: Generally 1 day when treasury actions flow through Level 1 / short executor governance.
Timelock bypass allowed: Yes / partially. Treasury custody is not purely timelock-only because FUNDS_ADMIN is a live privileged role on the treasury contract, and emergency / steward-style privileged actors exist elsewhere in the system.

metrics.name: Treasury custody integrity
metrics.desc:
Treasury is governance-linked, not held by a single opaque operator.
Treasury powers include direct fund movement capabilities via FUNDS_ADMIN.
The effective delay is only ~1 day for relevant actions, which is not a strong buffer for a $129M treasury.
Partial bypass / live privileged access means custody is not protected purely by slow governance execution.

metrics.name: Anchor scoring
metrics.desc:
Anchor,Score,Anchor PD
Governance integrity & liveness,1,0.10%
Control-plane integrity,3,2%
Treasury custody integrity,3,2%

metrics.name: Jurisdictional clarity
metrics.desc:
Critical controllers that are identifiable entities: Yes. Aave governance includes identifiable multisig-controlled roles such as the Aave Governance V3 Guardian, Emergency Admin, and Risk Council; DeFiScan notes Emergency Admin signers are considered insiders of the Aave DAO.
Documented sanctions: None found in available sources.

metrics.name: Jurisdictional clarity
metrics.desc:
Identifiable controllers exist, which weakens pure decentralization.
But there is no evidence of sanctions or jurisdictional action affecting governance today.

metrics.name: Governance power distribution and participation
metrics.desc: See anchor Governance integrity & liveness evidence. Key metrics: quorum hit rate 18/20, top 5 delegate concentration ~38.5%, top 10 concentration unknown.

metrics.name: Governance power distribution and participation
metrics.desc:
Participation is strong: quorum hit rate is 18/20.
But concentration is meaningful: top 5 delegates ~38.5%.
That supports a mild negative modifier, not a severe one.

metrics.name: Voting power integrity
metrics.desc:
Vote power source: AAVE, stkAAVE, and aAAVE balances on Ethereum mainnet; governance powers can be delegated jointly or separately.
Flash-loan resistance: Strong by design. Voting balances remain on Ethereum mainnet and are validated on voting networks using storage proofs rather than by moving funds to the voting chain.
Known delegation/wrapper edge cases: Wrapper complexity exists because voting power aggregates across AAVE, stkAAVE, and aAAVE and can also use cross-chain representatives, but no exploit is documented in available sources.

metrics.name: Voting power integrity
metrics.desc:
Voting power is tied to tracked balances on Ethereum mainnet, a good integrity property.
Cross-chain voting uses proofs, reducing simple manipulation risk.
Multi-wrapper and representative mechanics add some operational complexity.

metrics.name: Voting mechanism integrity
metrics.desc:
Mechanism: Snapshot for non-binding Temp Check / ARFC, then on-chain AIP voting through Governance v3.
Known past governance exploits or mitigations: No past governance exploit found; mitigations include quorum, vote differential, timelocks, modular governance, cross-chain validation through a.DI, and guardian cancellation during timelock.
Execution lifecycle: Forum → Snapshot → ARFC → AIP → on-chain vote → timelocked execution; short executor 1-day delay, long executor 7-day delay.

metrics.name: Voting mechanism integrity
metrics.desc:
The mechanism is mature and layered, with clear stages and execution delays.
Guardian cancellation and timelocks strengthen execution integrity.
Cross-chain governance adds complexity, so not best-in-class simplicity.

metrics.name: Governance parameters safeguards
metrics.desc:
Which path changes governance parameters: Governance executor path. DeFiScan states setVotingConfigs can change cooldown, voting duration, yes threshold, vote differential, and minimum proposition power.
Is it harder than normal proposals: Yes. Aave separates Level 2 permissions for governance-system changes from Level 1 permissions for most other actions, and long-executor / governance-permission changes carry a 7-day timelock.

metrics.name: Governance parameters safeguards
metrics.desc:
Governance parameters are not changed through the easiest path.
Extra separation and longer delay materially improve safety.
Still ultimately governance-controlled, so not immune to capture.

metrics.name: Protocol parameters safeguards
metrics.desc:
Which path changes protocol parameters: Primarily Level 1 governance, with some scoped steward / council roles under guardrails for reserve and risk parameters.
Highest-risk parameters: Reserve configuration items such as LTVs, liquidation thresholds/bonuses, interest-rate data, borrow caps, supply caps, reserve freeze/pause, and related pool settings.

metrics.name: Protocol parameters safeguards
metrics.desc:
Important protocol parameters can change through a relatively fast path.
Guardrails help, but live multisig/steward intervention increases trust assumptions.
This deserves a mild negative modifier.

metrics.name: Protocol upgrades safeguards
metrics.desc:
Upgrade path: Governance-controlled upgradeable contracts and payload execution through Governance v3 / execution networks.
Who can upgrade: Aave Governance controls upgrades for protected paths; DeFiScan notes critical permissions involve governance plus the Emergency Admin in some areas.
Time-to-upgrade: Typically 1 day for short-executor proposals and 7 days for long-executor / governance-permission proposals.

metrics.name: Protocol upgrades safeguards
metrics.desc:
Upgrades are not instant and are embedded in governance flow.
The 7-day path for higher-sensitivity permissions is a real safeguard.
The system remains upgradeable and not fully immutable.

metrics.name: Emergency roles risk
metrics.desc: See anchor Control-plane integrity evidence. Key detail: EMERGENCY_ADMIN / POOL_ADMIN can pause without going through normal proposal execution flow; DeFiScan explicitly flags this as a major centralization / exit-window weakness.

metrics.name: Emergency roles risk
metrics.desc:
Emergency roles can bypass normal governance flow for pause-related actions.
DeFiScan explicitly flags this as a major centralization / exit-window weakness.
This is one of the clearest negative modifiers in the setup.

metrics.name: Protocol complexity
metrics.desc:
Number of chains: 21
Number of pools/markets (or major modules): 20
Number of executors / timelocks: Two main executor levels with 1-day and 7-day timelocks.
Number of privileged roles: At least 8 named ACL roles, plus treasury and related privileged roles.
Change frequency: High; quorum hit rate 18/20 indicates active governance.

metrics.name: Protocol complexity
metrics.desc:
Aave governance is sophisticated, but complexity itself raises operational risk.
Cross-chain voting and execution, many roles, and multiple guarded paths make review harder.
This looks like a mild negative, not an extreme one.

metrics.name: Treasury custody risk
metrics.desc:
Treasury move friction: Moderate to low. FUNDS_ADMIN can approve, transfer, create streams, cancel streams, and withdraw from streams from the treasury collector.
Can a single proposal drain treasury: Potentially yes in practice, because treasury movement powers exist and relevant governance actions can flow through the short path with about 1-day timelock; treasury control is not purely timelock-only.

metrics.name: Treasury custody risk
metrics.desc:
Treasury movement powers are broad.
Delay is short relative to treasury value.
This is the strongest negative control modifier after emergency-role risk.

Number of independent audits: 20+

metrics.name: Audits
metrics.desc:
Very strong audit depth and independence.
No known unaudited in-scope surface.
This fits the strongest audit credit.

metrics.name: Formal verification
metrics.desc:
Formal verification performed: Yes, Certora Prover
Coverage scope: Protocol

metrics.name: Formal verification
metrics.desc:
Formal verification is present.
Coverage is described at protocol level, not just a narrow component.
This fits the strongest formal-methods credit.

metrics.name: Testing
metrics.desc:
Unit tests: Yes
Integration tests: Yes
Fork simulations: Yes
Invariant tests: Yes

metrics.name: Testing
metrics.desc:
Testing appears broad across unit, integration, fork, and invariant layers.
Invariant coverage is especially meaningful for protocol assurance.
Strong, but not stronger-than-formal, credit.

metrics.name: Monitoring
metrics.desc: Active monitoring/alerting vendor: Hypernative

metrics.name: Monitoring
metrics.desc:
Active third-party monitoring is in place.
This improves detection and response, though it does not prevent bugs by itself.

metrics.name: Incidents history
metrics.desc:
Major incidents affecting in-scope contracts in last 24 months: 0
Severity: None

metrics.name: Incidents history
metrics.desc:
No major recent incidents is a meaningful assurance signal.
It supports operational robustness, though absence of incidents is not proof of correctness.

metrics.name: Upgrades history
metrics.desc: Upgrade cadence: Some upgrades recorded; precise cadence not fully classified.

metrics.name: Upgrades history
metrics.desc:
Upgrade cadence is too vague to support positive or negative credit.
Left neutral pending more precise cadence classification.

metrics.name: Smart contract maturity
metrics.desc:
Deployed on: 2022
Stability: High

metrics.name: Smart contract maturity
metrics.desc:
Multi-year production history is a real maturity signal.
Good, but not maximum Lindy relative to older systems.
